name: Community National Bank & Trust of Texas
description_short: Community National Bank & Trust of Texas is a locally-focused community bank serving 18 Texas communities since 1964, offering personal and business checking, savings, and online banking services.
description_long:
Community National Bank & Trust of Texas (CNB&T) has been operating as a community bank since 1964, establishing itself as a neighborhood-oriented financial institution across Texas. The bank emphasizes personal relationships and local service, positioning itself as a hometown alternative to larger national banks. In June 2024, CNB&T expanded its footprint by acquiring CapTex Bank, integrating that customer base into their operations. The institution is FDIC-insured and maintains multiple branch locations across 18 communities in Texas.

CNB&T offers a comprehensive range of banking services including personal checking and savings accounts, business checking accounts, online banking, mobile banking through the MyBankTX app, and overdraft protection. The bank provides wire transfer services, ACH origination requests, and features like Round-Up Automatic Savings that rounds debit card transactions to the nearest dollar. They actively promote account opening with incentives such as free Level Up Sports Pickleball Sets for new checking account customers or referrals. The bank also offers CardSwap integration for updating payment subscriptions across 150+ online merchants.

CNB&T distinguishes itself through its explicit community focus and local decision-making approach. The bank markets itself on a 50+ year history of customer relationships and supporting small businesses when other lenders would not. They provide a financial resource library partnered with Banzai covering life events from college planning to retirement. The institution actively participates in community events including food bank donations and local founder's day celebrations across their service areas. This positions them distinctly against larger regional and national banks.

The primary limitation of CNB&T is geographic constraint—services are only available in their 18 Texas branch locations, making them inaccessible to customers outside these areas. The website provides limited transparency on specific product details, interest rates, and fee structures. While they emphasize online and mobile banking capabilities, the actual feature set and functionality compared to larger banks remains unclear from available information. The recent CapTex acquisition integration may create transition periods for affected customers.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: FDIC-insured with 60+ years of operational history since 1964
Serves 18 communities across Texas with local branch presence and personal service model
Free checking account incentives with new account openings or customer referrals
Round-Up Automatic Savings feature that rounds transactions to nearest dollar for savings
Mobile banking through MyBankTX app with 24/7 online account access
Free financial resource library partnered with Banzai covering all life stages
Active community involvement including food bank donations and local event sponsorships
Business checking accounts specifically designed to support small business owners
cons: Geographic limitation—only operates in 18 Texas communities, not accessible outside service areas
Website lacks transparent interest rates, APYs, and detailed fee schedules for products
Recent CapTex Bank acquisition (June 2024) may create ongoing system integration issues for merged customers
Limited product information available online regarding CDs, money market accounts, or savings account tiers
No information provided about loan products, credit cards, or mortgage services despite claiming comprehensive banking
best_for: Texas residents in CNB&T's 18 service communities seeking personal relationship-based banking
Small business owners needing dedicated business checking and local lender support
Customers prioritizing local community involvement and hometown bank service model
Individuals transitioning from CapTex Bank following the June 2024 acquisition
diagnosis: Community National Bank & Trust is best for Texas residents in their 18-community service area who prioritize local, relationship-based banking and community involvement over competitive rates and product breadth. The main caveat is strict geographic limitation—this bank is not accessible outside their Texas branch locations, and the website obscures specific rates and fees that should factor into any banking decision.
services: Personal checking accounts with free account opening promotions
Business checking accounts designed for small business support
Online banking platform with fund transfers and ACH origination
Mobile banking through MyBankTX app for 24/7 account access
CardSwap integration for payment subscription management across 150+ merchants
Financial resource library with Banzai partnership
Customer referral program with gift incentives
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
